mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect ongoing labor demand that supports corporate revenue growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not positive in >55% of months with positive PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 2.5% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy faster than markets discount, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with an unemployment rate below 4.5% signals restrictive policy applied to an overheated labor market, sharply elevating recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.5% while FEDFUNDS is above 1.5%
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE does not significantly predict negative returns (p > 0.05) or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, consecutive monthly declines in housing starts (HOUST) transmit rising mortgage rate pressures into the real economy, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns as residential investment contracts.
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles, HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving corporate operating leverage and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth is not associated with positive 5-day forward returns in >55% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals a contraction in consumer spending that reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS MoM declines are followed by negative 20-day S&P returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and declines in industrial production (INDPRO) jointly confirm a recessionary dynamic that single series miss, generating strongly negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over 3 months and INDPRO declines over same period
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE rises and INDPRO declines do not produce negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered episodes, or the interaction term adds less than 1% incremental R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, quarter-over-quarter annualized GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% signals that restrictive policy is successfully slowing the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as earnings estimates are cut.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth < 2% during tightening cycles is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).
